---
title: "FAQ and Common Issues"
description: "Find a familiar symptom, inspect one clue, and test a small fix without guessing."
execute:
  echo: true
categories: [faq, debugging, beginner]
order: 261
colab_notebook: "notebooks/lessons/faq/index.ipynb"
---
{{< include ../_includes/colab-link.qmd >}}

<!-- fcpython-chapter-lab: start -->
## How to use this chapter hands-on

Use this chapter when Python does something surprising and you are not sure
which lesson can help. Begin with the words that describe what you see, such as
"unexpected keyword argument," "nothing printed," "this name is undefined,"
or "Python will not add these values."

Do not apply every suggested fix at once. Open the Colab notebook, copy the
small example that best matches the symptom, and follow this routine:

1. Find the symptom that resembles the problem.
2. Predict one likely cause before applying a fix.
3. Run the smallest example and inspect the evidence.
4. Change one thing, then run the example again.
5. Explain what the result showed in one sentence.

By the end of a lesson, keep one small troubleshooting note with four parts:
**symptom**, **clue**, **change**, and **result**. That note becomes a reusable
debugging tool for future projects.
<!-- fcpython-chapter-lab: end -->

## Find your question

Choose the page whose questions sound most like your current problem.

- [Unexpected Keyword Arguments in Classes](unexpected-keyword-arguments-in-classes.qmd)
  - Why did my class reject a keyword argument?
  - How do class calls connect to `__init__`?
  - Why must an instance method begin with `self`?
  - How do positional and keyword arguments reach parameters?
- [Running Code and Understanding Output](running-code-and-output.qmd)
  - Why did nothing print?
  - Why can a notebook give an old or surprising result?
  - What is the difference between displaying, printing, and returning a value?
  - When should I restart the runtime?
- [Names, Values, and Types](names-values-and-types.qmd)
  - Why is my variable undefined?
  - Why did assigning or changing a name produce an unexpected value?
  - Why does `input()` return text?
  - Why can Python not combine a string and a number?

::: {.callout-tip title="Search with the symptom"}
Search for the words Python showed you and describe what happened, not only
what you hoped would happen. For example, search for `unexpected keyword
argument level`, `NameError undefined name`, or `input returns text` rather
than `my code is broken`.
:::

## Questions answered elsewhere

This FAQ provides short diagnostic paths. Use the main lessons when you need a
complete explanation and more practice.

| If you need help with... | Continue with... |
| --- | --- |
| A traceback or named exception | [Error Messages](../debugging/error-messages.qmd) |
| Inspecting a program while it runs | [Debugging Tools](../debugging/debugging-tools.qmd) |
| Making a small example to share | [Minimal Reproducible Examples](../debugging/minimal-reproducible-examples.qmd) |
| Classes, instances, methods, and `self` | [Object-Oriented Basics](../object-oriented-python/oop-basics.qmd) |
| Variables and Python types | [Values, Variables, and Types](../core-python/values-variables-types.qmd) |
| Text input and printed output | [Strings, Input, and Output](../core-python/strings-input-output.qmd) |
| Colab cells and runtimes | [Google Colab](../getting-started/google-colab.qmd) |

[Return to the full lesson path](../index.qmd) when the immediate problem is
solved.

## Choose the next useful step

::: {.quiz-box}
Use the evidence in each situation to choose one small diagnostic step.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "faq-overview",
  "title": "FAQ troubleshooting checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose the next small step, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "nothing-visible",
      "prompt": "A script runs without an error, but you see no output. What should you inspect first?",
      "options": [
        "Check whether the script reaches a print call or another output action.",
        "Reinstall Python immediately.",
        "Change every variable name.",
        "Delete the script and start over."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "A successful calculation can remain invisible. First check whether the program performs an output action."
    },
    {
      "id": "name-error",
      "prompt": "Python reports NameError for total_price. What is a useful first check?",
      "options": [
        "Check the spelling and whether total_price was assigned before this line ran.",
        "Convert every value to text.",
        "Add a loop around the program.",
        "Ignore the exact name in the message."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "NameError means Python cannot find that name at that moment, so spelling and execution order are strong first clues."
    },
    {
      "id": "one-change",
      "prompt": "Why should you change one thing before running the code again?",
      "options": [
        "The new result gives clearer evidence about whether that change mattered.",
        "Python only permits one edit per file.",
        "It guarantees that the first guess is correct.",
        "It prevents all future errors."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "A controlled change keeps cause and effect visible, even when the first guess is not the answer."
    },
    {
      "id": "unexpected-keyword",
      "prompt": "A class reports an unexpected keyword argument named level. What should you compare first?",
      "options": [
        "Compare level in the call with the parameters accepted by __init__.",
        "Convert every attribute to a list.",
        "Remove self from the initializer.",
        "Add **kwargs without inspecting the class."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "The error reports a mismatch between a supplied keyword and the active callable signature."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}

## Keep the FAQ useful

When you encounter a repeated question that is not covered here, record:

- the exact symptom or error message;
- the smallest code that shows it;
- the clue that identified the cause;
- the smallest change that fixed it; and
- the lesson that explains the underlying concept.

Questions with the same theme can become a cohesive FAQ lesson. A single
question that already has a full course lesson should become a short link rather
than duplicated material.
